title: NetForge: C++ Systems and Networking Interview Sprint

atomic_block_policy.note: Every listed block is core and counted toward its day duration. Optional stretch work stays outside this manifest so the published time budget remains honest.

completion_policy: A day is complete only when every core block is finished and the proof block's evidence exists. A passing command without retained evidence is not completion.

blocks.title: Retrieve stream-I/O invariants
blocks.action: Without notes, state how short reads, short writes, EINTR, EOF, declared length, and allocation order must be handled. Predict three boundary failures before opening the source.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-01/recall.md.

blocks.title: Run the practical baseline
blocks.action: Run scripts/diagnostic.sh from the red starter. Spend 20 minutes coding without help, then 10 minutes scoring the result and choosing the lower evidence-backed learning lane.
blocks.evidence: Diagnostic output and lane decision in capstone/work/evidence/day-01/diagnostic.txt.

blocks.title: Model a bounded frame
blocks.action: Draw the stream-to-header-to-bounded-payload state machine and label every point at which progress, EOF, error, or rejection occurs.
blocks.evidence: A frame-state sketch in capstone/work/evidence/day-01/frame-model.md.

blocks.title: Implement exact framed I/O
blocks.action: Implement the Day 1 TODOs in capstone/work/src/protocol.cpp: loop on progress, retry EINTR, distinguish EOF from error, decode byte order explicitly, and reject oversized lengths before allocation.
blocks.evidence: Compiling protocol.cpp plus the Day 1 diff and test transcript.

blocks.title: Attack the frame boundary
blocks.action: Exercise zero-byte EOF, a split header, a split payload, EINTR, and an oversized declaration; preserve the first failing input before fixing it.
blocks.evidence: One red-to-green boundary case in capstone/work/evidence/day-01/boundary-case.txt.

blocks.title: Prove Day 1
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day01 from a clean build and save the exact command, environment, and output.
blocks.evidence: Passing output in capstone/work/evidence/day-01/all-tests.txt.

blocks.title: Record the learning delta
blocks.action: Write the evidence-backed learning delta and next spaced retrieval date in the Day 1 session summary. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence demonstrates non-trivial understanding, corrects a misconception, confirms prior depth, or changes the mission.
blocks.evidence: capstone/work/evidence/day-01/session-summary.md, plus an optional learning-records entry only when the Teach threshold is met.

blocks.title: Retrieve ownership failure paths
blocks.action: List every exit path around a manual close, name which generated special members violate unique ownership, and explain why destructors must not throw.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book ownership answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-02/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model descriptor ownership
blocks.action: Draw a descriptor lifetime through create, move, release, reset, and destruction; mark exactly one owner at every transition.
blocks.evidence: Ownership transition table in capstone/work/evidence/day-02/ownership-model.md.

blocks.title: Build unique_fd
blocks.action: Complete unique_fd.hpp with deleted copy operations, noexcept move construction and assignment, release, reset, and an explicit self-move policy.
blocks.evidence: Compiling unique_fd.hpp and a focused Day 2 test transcript.

blocks.title: Break moves and cleanup
blocks.action: Test move assignment over an owned destination, self-move, release, reset-to-same, and scope exit after an error; use fcntl with F_GETFD and EBADF to prove closure.
blocks.evidence: Descriptor-liveness observations in capstone/work/evidence/day-02/failure-paths.txt.

blocks.title: Code an ownership-aware LRU
blocks.action: Complete the Day 2 LRU cache drill closed book, then annotate which container owns each node and which handles are non-owning.
blocks.evidence: Timed LRU source and ownership annotation in capstone/work/evidence/day-02/.

blocks.title: Defend the RAII choice
blocks.action: Record a two-minute answer to why shared_ptr<int> is the wrong socket abstraction, covering semantic ownership, release policy, cycles, and reference-count cost.
blocks.evidence: Audio/video or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-02/raii-defense.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 2
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day02, retain the output, and record the move-assignment tradeoff and next retrieval date. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing test output and capstone/work/evidence/day-02/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve vocabulary-type lifetimes
blocks.action: Explain when string_view dangles, what optional expresses beyond a sentinel, when variant beats inheritance, and who owns every returned byte.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-03/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model explicit parse states
blocks.action: Map each command and malformed state to a concrete C++20 type; mark view lifetimes and the point where borrowed bytes must become owned.
blocks.evidence: Parse-state and lifetime table in capstone/work/evidence/day-03/type-model.md.

blocks.title: Build bounded command parsing
blocks.action: Implement valid_key and parse_command for PING, STATS, SET, GET, and DEL while preserving spaces in SET values and returning explicit bounded errors.
blocks.evidence: Compiling parser source and focused Day 3 tests.

blocks.title: Break views and malformed input
blocks.action: Try empty input, unknown verbs, embedded NUL, extra arguments, invalid or 129-byte keys, empty values, and commands returned from temporary storage.
blocks.evidence: A malformed-input matrix in capstone/work/evidence/day-03/parser-cases.md.

blocks.title: Code CIDR membership
blocks.action: Complete the Day 3 CIDR membership drill closed book, including /0, /32, invalid prefix lengths, and explicit byte order.
blocks.evidence: Timed CIDR source and results in capstone/work/evidence/day-03/.

blocks.title: Defend the error boundary
blocks.action: Compare variant, exceptions, error codes, and C++23 expected for this parser; choose based on boundary and recoverability rather than novelty.
blocks.evidence: A two-minute recorded defense or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-03/error-policy.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 3
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day03, retain the output, and record one interface decision and next retrieval date. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing test output and capstone/work/evidence/day-03/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve memory-model facts
blocks.action: Define a data race, state what a mutex protects, explain spurious wakeups, and name the predicate transition that must precede notification.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-04/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model invariant and predicate
blocks.action: Write the bounded-queue invariant in one sentence, then list the producer, consumer, and close predicates and every state transition that can wake them.
blocks.evidence: Queue invariant table in capstone/work/evidence/day-04/queue-model.md.

blocks.title: Build the bounded queue
blocks.action: Implement BlockingQueue with finite capacity, one mutex, predicate waits, two condition variables, close, and drain semantics.
blocks.evidence: Compiling queue implementation and focused queue tests.

blocks.title: Build the synchronized store
blocks.action: Implement KvStore with one documented synchronization invariant and no unsynchronized observations of mutable shared state.
blocks.evidence: Compiling store implementation and its invariant comment.

blocks.title: Break close and capacity edges
blocks.action: Test capacity one, close while a producer waits, close while a consumer waits, and close with queued items; loop the smallest stress case.
blocks.evidence: Repeated stress output in capstone/work/evidence/day-04/stress.txt.

blocks.title: Code sliding-window maximum
blocks.action: Complete the Day 4 sliding-window maximum drill closed book, state the deque invariant, and cover duplicate values and window size one.
blocks.evidence: Timed drill source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-04/.

blocks.title: Explain happens-before
blocks.action: Draw and narrate the synchronizes-with and happens-before chain from producer mutation to consumer observation; explain why volatile cannot replace it.
blocks.evidence: Recorded whiteboard or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-04/happens-before.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 4
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day04 repeatedly, retain output, and record the close-policy tradeoff. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing repeated output and capstone/work/evidence/day-04/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve thread lifetime rules
blocks.action: State what happens when a joinable thread dies, which worker-visible objects must outlive workers, and why requesting stop does not interrupt every blocking syscall.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model accept-close-drain-join
blocks.action: Draw construction, task admission, shutdown, queue drain, worker exit, join, and member destruction in temporal order.
blocks.evidence: Thread-lifetime sequence in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/lifetime-model.md.

blocks.title: Build bounded workers
blocks.action: Complete thread_pool.cpp with N workers, bounded admission, worker-boundary exception containment, drain semantics, idempotent shutdown, and join.
blocks.evidence: Compiling pool implementation and focused Day 5 tests.

blocks.title: Measure task parallelism
blocks.action: Implement the bounded range partition in labs/parallelism/answer.cpp, run the checksum and scaling harness three times, and compare speedup, efficiency, Amdahl's ceiling, and adjacent-versus-padded counters without claiming causality from one run.
blocks.evidence: Runner output and an Amdahl/false-sharing interpretation in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/parallelism.txt and parallelism.md.

blocks.title: Break admission and shutdown
blocks.action: Exercise zero workers, capacity one, a throwing task, simultaneous submit and shutdown, double shutdown, and destruction with queued work.
blocks.evidence: Smallest failing interleaving and final result in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/shutdown-cases.txt.

blocks.title: Code a timer heap
blocks.action: Complete the Day 5 timer-heap drill closed book, including equal deadlines, cancellation policy, and monotonic-clock reasoning.
blocks.evidence: Timed timer-heap source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/.

blocks.title: Defend cancellation
blocks.action: Compare tasks with threads, cooperative cancellation with forced interruption, and waking a queue wait with interrupting a client blocked in recv.
blocks.evidence: Recorded answer or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-05/cancellation-defense.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 5
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day05 under repeated load, retain output, and record the shutdown-order decision. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing load output and capstone/work/evidence/day-05/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve packet envelopes
blocks.action: Draw Ethernet, optional 802.1Q, IPv4, and TCP in order; name the fields controlling the next offset, network byte order, and captured-versus-declared length checks.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book packet drawing in capstone/work/evidence/day-06/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model validate-before-decode
blocks.action: Create an offset ledger where every read is preceded by both capture-bound and protocol-declaration checks.
blocks.evidence: Offset ledger in capstone/work/evidence/day-06/offset-model.md.

blocks.title: Build the packet decoder
blocks.action: Implement hex parsing and decode_ethernet_ipv4_tcp with zero or one VLAN tag, explicit byte decoding, and precise bounded errors.
blocks.evidence: Compiling packet.cpp and focused Day 6 tests.

blocks.title: Break every header boundary
blocks.action: Truncate at each boundary and mutate EtherType, IP version, IHL, total length, protocol, TCP data offset, and VLAN fields.
blocks.evidence: Packet mutation matrix in capstone/work/evidence/day-06/mutations.md.

blocks.title: Code longest-prefix routing
blocks.action: Complete the Day 6 longest-prefix route lookup closed book and test overlapping routes, the default route, host routes, and no match.
blocks.evidence: Timed route-lookup source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-06/.

blocks.title: Trace one handshake
blocks.action: Capture one loopback handshake with tcpdump on Linux or macOS and identify SYN, SYN-ACK, and ACK from flags, ports, and sequence behavior.
blocks.evidence: Packet capture excerpt and annotated explanation in capstone/work/evidence/day-06/handshake.md.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 6
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day06, retain output, and record one validation-order decision. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing output and capstone/work/evidence/day-06/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve the socket lifecycle
blocks.action: Order resolution, socket, bind, listen, accept, connect, framed transfer, shutdown, and close; distinguish listener ownership from connected ownership and EOF from reset, timeout, EINTR, and EAGAIN.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book lifecycle in capstone/work/evidence/day-07/recall.md.

blocks.title: Build client and server lifecycle
blocks.action: Implement the highest-risk client.cpp and server.cpp paths: resolve, connect or bind/listen, ephemeral-port reporting, bounded accept, descriptor transfer, framed request/response, and clean stop.
blocks.evidence: A working PING plus SET/GET transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-07/session.txt.

blocks.title: Break socket failure paths
blocks.action: Select the three riskiest cases from wrong port, bind conflict, mid-header close, oversized frame, slow payload, overload, and SIGINT; preserve errno at the failing boundary.
blocks.evidence: Three bounded failure observations in capstone/work/evidence/day-07/socket-failures.md.

blocks.title: Reset before the mock
blocks.action: Leave the screen, hydrate, and return with no notes or source tabs except the Mock A fixture and timer.
blocks.evidence: Break start and return timestamps in capstone/work/evidence/day-07/session-log.md.

blocks.title: Mock A: reproduce and localize
blocks.action: Start the independent Mock A timer. Reproduce the deterministic frame faults, preserve the first failure, state one hypothesis, and localize the smallest responsible boundary without opening the solution track.
blocks.evidence: First half of the uninterrupted 60-minute Mock A recording and investigation notes.

blocks.title: Mock A: repair, defend, and score
blocks.action: Continue the same timer. Implement the smallest repair, rerun the fixture, explain ownership and stream semantics aloud, and score the attempt using the shared scorecard.
blocks.evidence: Second half of the recording, passing fixture output, patch, and timestamped Mock A scorecard.

blocks.title: Prove the socket path
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day07 from a fresh server and correlate one PING packet trace with connect, accept, request, response, and FIN application events.
blocks.evidence: Passing Day 7 output and annotated trace in capstone/work/evidence/day-07/.

blocks.title: Record the midpoint decision
blocks.action: Record the lower evidence-backed Mock A score and choose the one failure mode to retrieve again on Days 8, 10, and 14. Create a Teach learning record only if the mock corrected or demonstrated non-trivial understanding.
blocks.evidence: capstone/work/evidence/day-07/session-summary.md and a scheduled retrieval item; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve readiness semantics
blocks.action: Explain why readiness is permission to attempt I/O rather than proof a frame will complete, then retrieve the Day 7 failure selected for spacing.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book readiness answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-08/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model per-connection state
blocks.action: Draw listener, readable, writable, partial-frame, output-high-water, timeout, and close states for a persistent connection.
blocks.evidence: Connection-state diagram in capstone/work/evidence/day-08/event-model.md.

blocks.title: Build bounded readiness behavior
blocks.action: Make listener readiness, repeated frames on one persistent connection, frame limits, and the chosen overload response explicit in the work track.
blocks.evidence: Passing persistent-session tests and the corresponding source diff.

blocks.title: Reset before overload testing
blocks.action: Take a screen-free break, then return with the overload command and observation template ready.
blocks.evidence: Break start and return timestamps in capstone/work/evidence/day-08/session-log.md.

blocks.title: Force bounded overload
blocks.action: Hold more incomplete clients than the worker and queue capacity, run a PING during the hold, and record the bounded latency, rejection, or timeout your policy produces.
blocks.evidence: Reproducible overload commands and timing in capstone/work/evidence/day-08/overload.txt.

blocks.title: Code a circular byte buffer
blocks.action: Complete the Day 8 circular-buffer drill closed book, including wraparound, empty/full distinction, and partial consume/append.
blocks.evidence: Timed buffer source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-08/.

blocks.title: Explain the scaling alternative
blocks.action: Write readiness.md comparing current worker ownership with a full reactor, including per-connection state, high/low-water marks, timeouts, and why the experiment is not a throughput benchmark.
blocks.evidence: capstone/work/evidence/day-08/readiness.md.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 8
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day08, retain output, and record the overload-policy tradeoff. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing output and capstone/work/evidence/day-08/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve process-boundary ownership
blocks.action: Explain which descriptors fork duplicates, why each process closes its unused endpoint immediately, and how EOF depends on all write-end owners closing.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book IPC answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-09/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model parent-child framing
blocks.action: Draw socketpair creation, fork, endpoint closure, framed request, framed reply, shutdown, waitpid, and exit-status propagation.
blocks.evidence: IPC lifetime diagram in capstone/work/evidence/day-09/ipc-model.md.

blocks.title: Build framed fork IPC
blocks.action: Build the Day 9 helper using socketpair, fork, existing framing, immediate unused-end closure, deterministic parent/child behavior, and waitpid status checks.
blocks.evidence: Compiling day09-ipc and a successful round-trip transcript.

blocks.title: Break the tail and ownership
blocks.action: Run the torn-child-frame case under a two-second timeout and prove it terminates rather than hanging because inherited endpoints were closed correctly.
blocks.evidence: Timed break-tail result in capstone/work/evidence/day-09/break-tail.txt.

blocks.title: Code Top-K endpoints
blocks.action: Complete the Day 9 Top-K endpoint drill closed book, defining tie order, memory bound, and behavior when k exceeds the unique endpoint count.
blocks.evidence: Timed Top-K source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-09/.

blocks.title: Defend IPC choices
blocks.action: Compare socketpair with a pipe and shared memory for this framed exchange; include backpressure, ownership, serialization, and failure isolation.
blocks.evidence: Recorded answer or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-09/ipc-defense.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 9
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day09 and both manual IPC commands, retain outputs, and record the process-boundary decision. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing outputs and capstone/work/evidence/day-09/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve the evidence ladder
blocks.action: Without notes, order reproduction, preserved inputs, debugger or trace, one hypothesis, sanitizer choice, profiler choice, minimal repair, and regression proof; retrieve the Day 7 spaced failure again.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book debugging ladder in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/recall.md.

blocks.title: Localize the EBADF injection
blocks.action: Reproduce the injected EBADF path, then run the disposable ASan use-after-free and TSan data-race fixtures. Freeze edits, preserve first reports, inspect the failing boundary, test one hypothesis per fault, and make the smallest repairs in evidence copies.
blocks.evidence: Debugger transcript, rejected hypothesis, minimal EBADF patch, red/green ASan and TSan reports, and fixture diagnosis under capstone/work/evidence/day-10/.

blocks.title: Reset before clean builds
blocks.action: Take a screen-free break, then return with separate empty ASan/UBSan and TSan build directories.
blocks.evidence: Break start and return timestamps in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/session-log.md.

blocks.title: Build independent analysis lanes
blocks.action: Run static analysis, then configure and execute ASan/UBSan and TSan in separate clean builds; never combine ASan and TSan into one result.
blocks.evidence: Static-analysis, ASan/UBSan, and TSan logs in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/.

blocks.title: Test one profiler hypothesis
blocks.action: State one CPU or contention hypothesis, collect the smallest relevant profiler observation, and write what result would disprove the claim.
blocks.evidence: Profiler command, observation, and falsifier in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/profile.md.

blocks.title: Code a deadlock detector
blocks.action: Complete the Day 10 wait-for-graph cycle detector closed book and test disconnected graphs, a self-cycle, and multiple cycles.
blocks.evidence: Timed detector source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/.

blocks.title: Run the incident oral
blocks.action: Answer the shutdown-hang and later-CPU-spike prompt aloud, asking for missing evidence and naming observations that would reject the leading hypothesis.
blocks.evidence: Recorded answer or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-10/incident-oral.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 10
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day10, retain all independent lanes, and record one diagnostic decision. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing output and capstone/work/evidence/day-10/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve durability boundaries
blocks.action: Distinguish process buffers, kernel page cache, local stable storage, and NAS guarantees; state exactly when this server may acknowledge a mutation.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book durability answers in capstone/work/evidence/day-11/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model write-ahead recovery
blocks.action: Draw append, checksum and length validation, required sync, acknowledgment, restart scan, valid-prefix replay, and corrupt-tail stop policy.
blocks.evidence: WAL state machine in capstone/work/evidence/day-11/wal-model.md.

blocks.title: Build framed WAL append
blocks.action: Implement a bounded framed WAL record and append path that completes the required durability sync before success is returned.
blocks.evidence: Compiling WAL append path and focused tests.

blocks.title: Build restart recovery
blocks.action: Implement restart scanning and deterministic valid-prefix recovery without trusting truncated lengths or corrupt records.
blocks.evidence: Restarted SET/GET transcript and recovery test output.

blocks.title: Break the durable tail
blocks.action: Inject a torn header, torn payload, oversized declaration, and corrupted record; verify bounded startup and the documented prefix policy.
blocks.evidence: Recovery fault matrix in capstone/work/evidence/day-11/recovery-cases.md.

blocks.title: Code WAL compaction
blocks.action: Complete the Day 11 WAL compaction drill closed book, preserving the latest value per key and specifying crash-safe replacement order.
blocks.evidence: Timed compaction source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-11/.

blocks.title: Defend the NAS boundary
blocks.action: Explain where local fsync assumptions stop at an NFS or SMB boundary and which server, cache, failover, and fencing facts must be established before promising durability.
blocks.evidence: Recorded answer or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-11/nas-boundary.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 11
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day11 plus a manual restart, retain output, and record the acknowledgment contract. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing output and capstone/work/evidence/day-11/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve hostile-boundary rules
blocks.action: List byte, time, work, queue, and state limits; explain why declared lengths are rejected before allocation and why parser acceptance is not authorization.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book boundary rules in capstone/work/evidence/day-12/recall.md.

blocks.title: Model the abuse budget
blocks.action: Map each untrusted input to its validation, resource budget, timeout, state transition, log signal, and rejection behavior.
blocks.evidence: Abuse-budget table in capstone/work/evidence/day-12/boundary-model.md.

blocks.title: Build boundary limits
blocks.action: Enforce protocol byte limits, connection and work bounds, timeout policy, valid state transitions, and safe error reporting at the earliest trusted boundary.
blocks.evidence: Compiling hardened path and focused Day 12 tests.

blocks.title: Build a fuzzable parser target
blocks.action: Compile the provided parser fuzz target with a bounded corpus and deterministic resource limits, keeping the command reproducible.
blocks.evidence: Fuzz target command and seed corpus under capstone/work/evidence/day-12/corpus/.

blocks.title: Minimize one hostile input
blocks.action: Run the target, preserve any crash or timeout, minimize one interesting input, and add it as a named regression even if the code already rejects it.
blocks.evidence: Minimized input and regression result in capstone/work/evidence/day-12/.

blocks.title: Code a safe varint
blocks.action: Complete the Day 12 variable-length integer drill closed book, covering truncation, overflow, excessive continuation bytes, and non-canonical encodings.
blocks.evidence: Timed varint source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-12/.

blocks.title: Explain layered containment
blocks.action: Walk one hostile frame from admission through parsing, work scheduling, storage, logging, and response; name the independent limit at each layer.
blocks.evidence: Recorded threat walk or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-12/containment.*.

blocks.title: Prove and record Day 12
blocks.action: Run ./scripts/check.sh work day12 plus ASan/UBSan, retain the fuzz result, and record one rejection-policy tradeoff. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: Passing outputs and capstone/work/evidence/day-12/session-summary.md; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve system-design anchors
blocks.action: Without notes, retrieve requirements, capacity math, invariants, flow, overload, consistency, recovery, observability, and one explicit tradeoff for the selected specialist branch.
blocks.evidence: Closed-book design skeleton in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/recall.md.

blocks.title: Build the branch design packet
blocks.action: Choose secure networking, NAS/file protocols, L2/L3 control and data plane, Unix/payments IPC, or low latency. Write requirements, back-of-envelope math, invariants, failure domains, and one request flow before recording.
blocks.evidence: Branch design packet in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/design-packet.md.

blocks.title: Record Mock C
blocks.action: Run one uninterrupted visible-timer Mock C attempt, using the base prompt and adapting only the final five minutes to the selected specialist branch. Do not consult notes or AI.
blocks.evidence: Uninterrupted recording or transcript plus timestamped Mock C scorecard.

blocks.title: Reset before coding
blocks.action: Leave the screen, hydrate, and return with only the Day 13 coding prompt and timer visible.
blocks.evidence: Break start and return timestamps in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/session-log.md.

blocks.title: Code failure-domain placement
blocks.action: Complete the Day 13 placement drill closed book, enforce capacity and anti-affinity, and explain what happens when constraints cannot all be satisfied.
blocks.evidence: Timed placement source and tests in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/.

blocks.title: Repair the weakest design branch
blocks.action: Review the lower evidence-backed Mock C subscores and re-explain only the weakest branch, naming the missing measurement or failure policy.
blocks.evidence: Focused re-explanation in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/debrief.md.

blocks.title: Prove the design packet
blocks.action: Verify that requirements, math, invariants, overload, failure domains, recovery, and observable signals are all present and linked to the Mock C scorecard.
blocks.evidence: Completed Day 13 proof checklist in capstone/work/evidence/day-13/proof.md.

blocks.title: Record the readiness risk
blocks.action: Record exactly one evidence-backed design risk for the Day 14 debrief. Create a Teach learning record only if the design evidence demonstrates or corrects non-trivial understanding.
blocks.evidence: capstone/work/evidence/day-13/session-summary.md with the named readiness risk; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.

blocks.title: Retrieve three readiness risks
blocks.action: Speak three closed-book answers: one ownership failure, one concurrency shutdown failure, and the Day 13 specialist-design risk. State the observable proof that would clear each risk.
blocks.evidence: Three spoken answers or transcript in capstone/work/evidence/day-14/recall.*.

blocks.title: Run the clean correctness matrix
blocks.action: From a known revision, run the full work-track build and CTest matrix with finite timeouts, then preserve the exact environment and commands.
blocks.evidence: Environment, revision, commands, and all-tests log in capstone/work/evidence/day-14/.

blocks.title: Run independent analysis lanes
blocks.action: Run ASan/UBSan, TSan, and static analysis in their documented clean lanes; stop and classify any failure instead of treating partial success as readiness.
blocks.evidence: Separate ASan/UBSan, TSan, and static-analysis logs in capstone/work/evidence/day-14/.

blocks.title: Establish the latency baseline
blocks.action: Use the persistent-session benchmark driver for three comparable runs with a warmup; record p50, p95, p99, maximum, environment, and a variance note without making a causal claim.
blocks.evidence: Three-run benchmark.txt and variance note in capstone/work/evidence/day-14/.

blocks.title: Mock 1: implement under timer
blocks.action: Start one uninterrupted 60-minute no-aid timer. Choose the general path and implement the TTL cache, or choose the low-latency path and implement the exact file line-reversal task without reading runner source or reference code.
blocks.evidence: First half of the Mock 1 recording and the timed source tree.

blocks.title: Mock 1: test, defend, and score
blocks.action: Continue the same timer. Run allowed black-box tests, handle boundary cases, give the sockets/concurrency oral or memory-and-I/O defense, then complete one timestamped scorecard.
blocks.evidence: Second half of the recording, results, defense notes, and evidence-backed Mock 1 scorecard.

blocks.title: Mock 2: specialist system design
blocks.action: Run one uninterrupted Mock D specialist prompt with no aid. Score it once as implementer and once as skeptical reviewer, retain the lower score, and mark self-assessed results provisional unless an independent interviewer scores them.
blocks.evidence: Mock 2 recording or transcript plus two timestamped scorecards and the retained lower score.

blocks.title: Audit the proof bundle
blocks.action: Check that correctness, sanitizers, static analysis, benchmark, selected coding path, two recordings, two scorecards, and debrief artifacts exist; do not pass a gate on confidence alone.
blocks.evidence: Completed authoritative checklist in capstone/work/evidence/day-14/proof.md.

blocks.title: Record the honest readiness verdict
blocks.action: Write debrief.md. Normalize each score only to its scoped rubric, keep self-scored mocks provisional, name the top residual risk, and schedule a peer-scored repeat after a spaced break if either loop is below 80 or lacks independent scoring. Create a Teach learning record only if the evidence meets its non-trivial-learning threshold.
blocks.evidence: debrief.md and a dated peer-repeat plan when required; a Teach learning record is optional and evidence-gated.
